description: Omission probes for the comprehensiveness judge: one shared note set and a faithful base summary, plus variants each deleting exactly ONE clinically significant fact. A good comprehensiveness judge flags the planted omission in each variant and flags nothing in the control.

notes.text: 58yo male with type 2 diabetes and hypertension for annual exam. Feels well overall. Reports good medication adherence. Exam: BP 142/88, otherwise unremarkable. Plan: colonoscopy referral placed (age 58, never screened — overdue); recheck A1c in 3 months; continue current medications.
Metformin 1000 mg PO BID. Lisinopril 20 mg PO daily. Atorvastatin 40 mg PO nightly. ALLERGIES: Penicillin — anaphylaxis.
A1c 8.2% (HIGH; prior 7.1% six months ago) — worsening glycemic control. eGFR 58 mL/min (CKD stage 3a). LDL 88 mg/dL.

probes.summary: 58-year-old male with type 2 diabetes and hypertension seen for his annual exam. Glycemic control has worsened, with A1c rising to 8.2% from 7.1% six months ago; a repeat A1c is planned in 3 months. He has stage 3a chronic kidney disease (eGFR 58). Medications are metformin 1000 mg twice daily, lisinopril 20 mg daily, and atorvastatin 40 mg nightly. He has a penicillin allergy with anaphylaxis. Blood pressure was elevated at 142/88. A colonoscopy referral was placed as he is overdue for screening.
